steps.title: Plan Your Setup
steps.subtitle: Understand your choices before you commit
steps.description: Choose your signing device, seed generation method, wallet software, and backup approach. Take your time here. The decisions you make in this step shape everything that follows.

steps.title: Set Up Your Hardware
steps.subtitle: Get your devices ready before creating any keys
steps.description: Unbox, verify, and configure your signing device. If you are using physical tools for seed generation (Seed Picker Cards, Entropia Pills, Codex32 volvelles), get those ready too.

steps.title: Create Your Key
steps.subtitle: Generate your Bitcoin seed phrase
steps.description: This is the most security-critical step. Work in a private space with no cameras. Never photograph your seed words. Never type them into a computer or phone.

steps.title: Put Your Key to Work
steps.subtitle: Set up your wallet, send and receive Bitcoin
steps.description: Connect your signing device to wallet software, export your public key, and make your first transaction. Test your backup before loading real funds.

steps.title: Protect Your Key
steps.subtitle: Secure your seed for the long term
steps.description: Stamp your seed into steel, consider a passphrase for additional security, and plan for inheritance. Your Bitcoin should outlast you.

seedsigner.name: SeedSigner / SeedSigner+
seedsigner.summary: DIY, stateless, air-gapped. 100% open source. Build from parts or buy pre-built.

prebuilt.label: Pre-Built (SeedSigner+)
steps: Insert the pre-loaded MicroSD card (if not already inserted)
Connect USB power cable
Wait 30-60 seconds for boot
Verify main menu appears on display

diy.label: DIY Build
steps: Download the latest SeedSigner OS image from github.com/SeedSigner/seedsigner/releases
Verify the SHA256 hash matches the published release hash
Flash the .img file to a MicroSD card using Balena Etcher or Raspberry Pi Imager
Wait for verification to complete (do not skip)
Solder GPIO headers to Pi Zero if not pre-soldered
Attach Waveshare 1.3" LCD Hat onto GPIO pins
Connect Pi Camera via ribbon cable
Insert flashed MicroSD card
Fit into 3D-printed case (if using one)
Connect USB power cable and verify boot

setup.note: Use Pi Zero v1.3 specifically. It has NO WiFi or Bluetooth hardware. The chips physically do not exist. Air-gap by hardware design.

coldcard-mk4.name: ColdCard Mk4
coldcard-mk4.summary: Compact, discreet, longest track record. Dual secure elements. MicroSD-only communication.

steps: Inspect the tamper-evident bag for cuts, tears, or signs of resealing
Note the bag number on the barcode label
Pull the blue tab to open (the word VOID should appear on the seal)
Verify the serialized tear-off tab width and perforations match the bag
Power on via USB-C to a wall charger (not a computer)
Accept Terms of Sale
Set your primary PIN (avoid obvious patterns)
Set your secondary PIN
Verify the bag number matches what the device displays
Update firmware via MicroSD if a newer version is available
setup.note: If anything about the packaging looks wrong, do not use the device. Contact support@coinkite.com with photos.

coldcard-q.name: ColdCard Q
coldcard-q.summary: Full QWERTY keyboard, camera, large color display. Flagship signing device with NFC and battery.

steps: Inspect the tamper-evident bag for cuts, tears, or signs of resealing
Note the bag number on the barcode label
Pull the blue tab to open (the word VOID should appear on the seal)
Verify the serialized tear-off tab width and perforations match the bag
Power on via USB-C or internal battery
Accept Terms of Sale
Set your primary PIN using the QWERTY keyboard
Set your secondary PIN
Verify the bag number matches what the device displays
Update firmware via MicroSD if a newer version is available
setup.note: The Q has a built-in camera for QR scanning and a rechargeable battery for fully portable, air-gapped operation.

passport.name: Foundation Passport
passport.summary: Premium machined aluminum. QR air-gap. Supply chain validation built in.

steps: Inspect packaging for signs of tampering
Power on the device
Run supply chain validation (device scans barcode and checks against Foundation's server)
If validation passes, proceed. If it fails, contact Foundation support.
Set your PIN
Update firmware if prompted
setup.note: Supply chain validation requires a one-time internet connection (via your phone scanning a QR). After this, the device operates fully air-gapped.

jade.name: Blockstream Jade / Jade Plus
jade.summary: Budget-friendly, travel-sized. Virtual secure element. QR-only mode on Jade Plus.

steps: Power on via USB-C
Choose setup mode: QR-only air-gap (Jade Plus) or USB/Bluetooth
Set your PIN
Device connects to Blockstream PIN server for initial key setup
Firmware update if available
setup.note: Jade uses a virtual secure element. Your seed is encrypted on the device and requires a blinding key from the Blockstream PIN server (or your own server) to decrypt. The server never sees your seed.

gen-hardware-wallet.name: Hardware Wallet RNG
gen-hardware-wallet.summary: Let your device generate the seed. Easiest method, requires trust in the manufacturer's random number generator.

gen-seed-picker.name: Seed Picker Cards
gen-seed-picker.summary: Shuffle a physical deck of BIP-39 word cards. Verifiable randomness, no electronics needed for generation.

gen-dice-rolls.name: Dice Rolls
gen-dice-rolls.summary: 99 dice rolls for maximum verifiable entropy. You control every bit of randomness.

gen-camera-entropy.name: Camera Entropy
gen-camera-entropy.summary: SeedSigner captures a photo and hashes it for entropy. Fast, open source, and unique to SeedSigner.

gen-entropia-pills.name: Entropia Pills
gen-entropia-pills.summary: 3D-printed capsules containing BIP-39 words. Tactile, educational, and verifiable.

gen-codex32.name: Codex32
gen-codex32.summary: Generate, verify, and split your seed entirely by hand using pen, paper, dice, and volvelles. Zero electronic trust.

sparrow-wallet.name: Sparrow Wallet
sparrow-wallet.summary: Desktop coordinator for single-sig and multisig. Coin control, PSBT signing, privacy tools.

bull-bitcoin.name: Bull Bitcoin
bull-bitcoin.summary: Non-custodial mobile wallet. Buy, sell, and spend Bitcoin without giving up your keys.

steel-single.name: Steel Backup (Single-Sig)
steel-single.summary: Stamp your 24 words into a steel plate. Fire-proof, flood-proof, forever.

steel-multi.name: Steel Backup (Multi-Sig)
steel-multi.summary: Seed phrases plus wallet descriptor on distributed steel plates. 2-of-3 recovery.

backup-verification.summary: Prove your backup works before loading real funds

inheritance-planning.summary: Make sure your Bitcoin outlives you

devices.summary: All 5 signing devices compared side by side
